page_content:
Corequisite: ARCH400.

Restriction: Must be in Architecture (Master's) program; and must be in the 3.5 year M. ARCH program.

ARCH445 Visual Analysis of Architecture (3 Credits)

Study of visual principles of architectural and urban precedents through graphic analysis. Exercises include on-site observation, documentation and analysis. Focuses on the development of an architect's sketchbook as a tool for life-long learning.

Prerequisite: ARCH400; or permission of ARCH-Architecture Program. Restriction: Must be in Architecture (B.S.) program.

ARCH448 Selected Topics in Visual Studies in Architecture (3 Credits) Selected Topics in Visual Studies in Architecture

Restriction: Permission of ARCH-Architecture Program; and must be ina major in ARCH-Architecture Program.

Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.
